page: Amazon Web Services m5n.16xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The instance provides 64 dedicated vCPUs across 32 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance, including single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, falls into the lower 25% tier. However, multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded decompression scale to average levels. Notably, the instance demonstrates strong memory performance, with large-block read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks reaching the top 25% tier. Extended CPU instruction tests also register in the top 25%, aided by hardware support for AVX-512 vector and neural network instructions.

With a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps, the m5n.16xlarge is engineered for network-heavy and EBS-intensive workloads. Qualitative cost efficiency is driven by its high resource density and robust memory throughput, making it suitable for distributed enterprise applications, database hosting, and web serving. It is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, redis caching, and prompt processing for small to medium language models, though it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or local-storage-dependent operations.
description: Amazon Web Services m5n.16xlarge is a network-optimized, general-purpose instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz, paired with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on its high-speed 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external storage and communication. Benchmarks show average multi-core CPU performance and weak single-core performance, but strong memory bandwidth for large-block reads and writes. It also benefits from AVX-512 instruction support. This hardware profile makes the m5n.16xlarge highly suitable for network-intensive applications, multi-threaded enterprise workloads, database operations, and web serving. The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is driven by its high network throughput and memory capacity, which support demanding multi-threaded tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.16xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, optimized for database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.16xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A network-optimized general-purpose instance featuring high-speed memory bandwidth and dedicated multi-core processing for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8259CL at 3.1 GHz
256.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong large-block read/write bandwidth
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and network-heavy tasks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Supports AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector operations
Best suited for multi-threaded enterprise applications, databases, and web serving
